summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, CS education / AI policy, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are Iran says strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ but sounds warning on US blockade, Lewd messages linked to University of Michigan regent who led campaign against pro-Palestinian students, and Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces.

mainItems.title: Iran says strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ but sounds warning on US blockade
mainItems.summary: Iran’s parliamentary speaker says strait could close again if US blockade continues, but Trump says it will remain in place until ‘transaction’ with Tehran is complete Iran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Lewd messages linked to University of Michigan regent who led campaign against pro-Palestinian students
mainItems.summary: Jordan Acker, who pushed legal action against protesters, is running for re-election in race reflecting tensions on Israel The University of Michigan regent Jordan Acker, who helped lead the university’s crackdown on pro-Palestinian…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
mainItems.summary: The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Lead prosecutor on investigation into former CIA director no longer on case
mainItems.summary: Maria Medetis Long had expressed doubts over case of John Brennan, whose agency said Russia boosted Trump in 2016 Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A federal prosecutor leading the investigation…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
mainItems.summary: Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
mainItems.summary: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: ‘Almost like a Bond villain’: why Labour MPs expect Starmer to cling on as PM
mainItems.summary: While the Labour leader is deeply unpopular, several factors – including the Iran war – seem to be delaying his exit It still feels improbable that the UK prime minister, Keir Starmer, will face a formal challenge even if, as assumed, his…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: "It's been an interesting year": the jailed creator of Fortune's Run has passed parole and is back working on her Deus Ex-style FPS
mainItems.summary: Last January, the lead developer of very good immersive sim shooter Fortune's Run revealed that she was going to prison for three years , having been convicted of an unspecified violent crime several years prior.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Maryland District Sheds Remedial High School Math Courses, Sees Students Soar
mainItems.summary: Administrators at Maryland’s Calvert County Public Schools believed the math classes they added to their course catalog years ago — pre-algebra and business math among them — helped students by giving them more time to master basic…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: After a month of haggling with players, Mega Crit make Slay the Spire 2's beta patches official, with big character reworks alongside new art and scoring systems
mainItems.summary: Slay the Spire 2 has received a major update that rolls all of the additions, tweaks and fixes from recent beta updates into the main branch of the game - though developers Mega Crit are at pains to repeat that "this is still an Early…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Useful if you care about how games actually work as designed experiences.

mainItems.title: Why scientists are nervous about fungi
mainItems.summary: They can pose a threat to human health — yeast infections are but one example. Scientists say not enough attention is paid to their ability to develop resistance to medications that treat them.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: What to Watch and Read This Weekend: Details, Details, Details (and an Intriguing Hamlet)
mainItems.summary: Though this be madness, yet there is method in't. The post What to Watch and Read This Weekend: Details, Details, Details (and an Intriguing Hamlet) appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: The Destroyed Remnants of a Lost World Are Falling to Earth, Scientists Discover
mainItems.summary: A rare class of meteorites called angrites likely come from a strange protoplanet that was catastrophically destroyed in the early solar system, leaving only fragmentary remnants.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
